outlook.headline: Industrial demand is recovering, but not uniformly
outlook.summary: Industrial demand looks better over the next 1-2 quarters, led by analog, distribution, and industrial-adjacent suppliers calling out broad-based growth, sequential recovery, and healthy pipelines. But a few names still see softness or only normalization, and the latest mention trend has eased slightly, so this reads more like an uneven rebound than a clean breakout.

drivers.label: Broad-based recovery at distributors and analog suppliers
drivers.detail: Avnet, Arrow, Texas Instruments, Analog Devices, Amkor, and Microchip all point to stronger industrial orders, with double-digit sequential growth in some cases and TI/ADI describing especially strong momentum.

drivers.label: Industrial design wins and pipeline building
drivers.detail: AMD, Advanced Energy, DuPont, CEVA, and Cadence describe new wins, improving bookings, and a building pipeline across industrial-related use cases, supporting further near-term demand.

drivers.label: Healthy specialty industrial/capital-equipment backdrop
drivers.detail: MKS Instruments, Air Products, KLA, and Ultra Clean Holdings all cite steady or broad-based activity, healthy backlog, and sustained investment, which helps keep industrial demand constructive.

drivers.label: Residual pockets of weakness and normalization
drivers.detail: Linde, Element Solutions, Renesas, GlobalFoundries, and Teradyne still describe weaker demand, flat industrial solutions, inventory buildup, or a rebound that has not fully arrived, limiting the pace of improvement.

risks: Industrial strength stays concentrated in a few suppliers and does not broaden into weaker names like Renesas, Teradyne, or Element Solutions.
EMEA or other regional weakness persists, especially where Linde flagged a softer industrial and chemicals environment.
Inventory normalization stalls or reverses before distributor and OEM order momentum fully converts into end-demand.
